segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I'm very pleased to welcome this evening's guest speaker, Mark Logan, who's going to tell us about the recent transformation of Minster Park.
segments.zh: 非常高兴欢迎今晚的嘉宾演讲者马克·洛根，他将向我们介绍明斯特公园最近的改造。有请，马克。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Over to you, Mark.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Thank you.
segments.zh: 谢谢。相信大家对明斯特公园都很熟悉。它已成为这座城市一大特色一个多世纪了，其中大部分时间由市议会负责管理。而或许鲜为人知的是这座公园的起源：与许多起初属于私人产业的公园不同——比如某座大宅的花园——明斯特原本是一块荒地，是1892年附近的居民开始在这里种花的。当时并不清楚这块地究竟归谁所有，这一问题直到20年后才得到解决，市议会随后接管了这块土地。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: I'm sure you're all familiar with Minster Park.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: It's been a feature of the city for well over a century, and has been the responsibility of the city council for most of that time.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: What perhaps isn't so well known is the origin of the park: unlike many public parks that started in private ownership, as the garden of a large house, for instance, Minster was some waste land, which people living nearby started planting with flowers in 1892.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: It was unclear who actually owned the land, and this wasn't settled until 20 years later, when the council took possession of it.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: You may have noticed the statue near one of the entrances.
segments.zh: 你们可能注意到其中一个入口附近的那座雕像。那是黛安·戈斯福思，她在公园的历史上扮演了关键角色。当市议会成为法定所有者后，曾打算把这块地卖掉盖房。许多当地居民希望它继续作为公共场所，让大家都能来这里呼吸新鲜空气、亲近自然——毕竟这座公园地处人口密集的居民区。黛安·戈斯福思正是其中一员，她组织了请愿和示威活动，最终迫使市议会改变了对这块地的处置计划。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: It's of Diane Gosforth, who played a key role in the history of the park.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Once the council had become the legal owner, it planned to sell the land for housing.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Many local people wanted it to remain a place that everyone could go to, to enjoy the fresh air and natural environment – remember the park is in a densely populated residential area.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Diane Gosforth was one of those people, and she organised petitions and demonstrations, which eventually made the council change its mind about the future of the land.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Soon after this the First World War broke out, in 1914, and most of the park was dug up and planted with vegetables, which were sold locally.
segments.zh: 在此之后不久，1914年第一次世界大战爆发，公园大部分区域被翻整并种上了蔬菜，就地销售。军队一度曾考虑征用这里作为部队训练场，甚至已经和市议会取得了联系，但最终判定公园面积太小、派不上用场。战争期间，公园里保留下来的一块草地上偶尔会举行公众集会。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: At one stage the army considered taking it over for troop exercises and got as far as contacting the city council, then decided the park was too small to be of use.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: There were occasional public meetings during the war, in an area that had been retained as grass.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: After the war, the park was turned back more or less to how it had been before 1914, and continued almost unchanged until recently.
segments.zh: 战后，公园大致恢复到了1914年前的模样，此后基本没什么变化，直到最近。曾多次拟定过改造方案，最近的一次是在2013年，不过在2015年动工之前又被修改了。真正的改造在2016年才最终启动，去年按期完工。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Plans for transforming it were drawn up at various times, most recently in 2013, though they were revised in 2015, before any work had started.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: The changes finally got going in 2016, and were finished on schedule last year.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: OK, let me tell you about some of the changes that have been made – and some things that have been retained.
segments.zh: 好，现在我来讲讲已经做了哪些改动，又保留了哪些东西。请大家看一下这张地图，你们能看到公园熟悉的轮廓：河流构成了北侧的边界，其余三面墙上各有一座大门。黛安·戈斯福思的雕像被挪了位置：以前紧挨着南门，现在移到了睡莲池的正北方，几乎在公园正中央，这样就显眼得多。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: If you look at this map, you'll see the familiar outline of the park, with the river forming the northern boundary, and a gate in each of the other three walls.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: The statue of Diane Gosforth has been moved: it used to be close to the south gate, but it's now immediately to the north of the lily pond, almost in the centre of the park, which makes it much more visible.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: There's a new area of wooden sculptures, which are on the river bank, where the path from the east gate makes a sharp bend.
segments.zh: 还新增了一片木雕区，位于河岸边，就在从东门过来的那条小路急转弯的地方。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: There are two areas that are particularly intended for children.
segments.zh: 有两片区域专门为孩子设计。游乐场经过扩建和翻新，位于河流和从池塘通往河边的小路之间。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: The playground has been enlarged and improved, and that's between the river and the path that leads from the pond to the river.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Then there's a new maze, a circular series of paths, separated by low hedges.
segments.zh: 接着是一座新的迷宫，由一系列环形小径构成，中间以低矮的树篱隔开。它就在西门附近——从西门往北朝河边方向走，然后左转就能到。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: That's near the west gate – you go north from there towards the river and then turn left to reach it.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: There have been tennis courts in the park for many years, and they've been doubled, from four to eight.
segments.zh: 网球场在公园里已经存在很多年了，现在数量从4片扩到了8片。它们仍旧在公园的西南角，也就是小路呈直角转弯的地方。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: They're still in the south-west corner of the park, where there's a right-angle bend in the path.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Something else I'd like to mention is the new fitness area.
segments.zh: 还有一件事想说，就是新建的健身区。它就在睡莲池边，和西门在同一侧。

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: This is right next to the lily pond on the same side as the west gate.

segments.speaker: MARK
segments.en: Now, as you're all gardeners, I'm sure you'll like to hear about the plants that have been chosen for the park.
segments.zh: 既然大家都是园艺爱好者，我想你们一定想听听公园选种了哪些植物。

questions.title: Questions 11–14
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.prompt:
The park was originally established
A  as an amenity provided by the city council.
B  as land belonging to a private house.
C  as a shared area set up by the local community.

pairs.p: unlike many public parks that started in private ownership
pairs.note: 题干问最初是怎么建立的，原文用 unlike 排除私人所有的选项，暗示明斯特是另一种起源。

pairs.q: set up by the local community
pairs.p: people living nearby started planting with flowers in 1892
pairs.note: 选项 C 的“由本地居民/社区自发设立”对应“附近居民开始种花”，故答案为 C。

paraphrase.explain: 原文说明公园不像其他公园那样从私产（如大宅花园）演变而来，而是本地居民1892年开始在荒地上种花，即社区自发设立，选 C。

items.prompt:
Why is there a statue of Diane Gosforth in the park?
A  She was a resident who helped to lead a campaign.
B  She was a council member responsible for giving the public access.
C  She was a senior worker at the park for many years.

pairs.note: 题干和原文同名，直接定位。
组织请愿和示威即领导一场运动，对应选项 A。

paraphrase.explain: Diane Gosforth 是当地居民之一，她组织了请愿和示威，让市议会改变了卖地计划，即帮助领导了一场运动，选 A。

items.prompt:
During the First World War, the park was mainly used for
A  exercises by troops.
B  growing vegetables.
C  public meetings.

pairs.p: the First World War broke out, in 1914
pairs.note: 题干时间点即一战期间，原文明确点出1914年一战爆发。
grow 与 plant 同义，均指种植蔬菜；且原文说“大部分公园被种上蔬菜”即主要用途为种菜。

paraphrase.explain: 一战期间公园大部分区域被翻整并种上了蔬菜；军队考虑训练但放弃；公众集会只是偶尔在一小块保留的草地上。因此主要用途是种菜，选 B。

items.prompt:
When did the physical transformation of the park begin?
A  2013
B  2015
C  2016

pairs.note: physical transformation 对应“改造真正启动”。
get going = begin/start，2016 年才真正开始。

paraphrase.explain: 2013 是拟定方案，2015 是修订方案（尚未动工），2016 才真正开始改造，选 C。

questions.title: Questions 15–20
instructions: Write the correct letter, A–I, next to Questions 15–20.

pairs.p: immediately to the north of the lily pond, almost in the centre of the park
pairs.note: 位于睡莲池正北、公园中心附近，对应地图上标记 E 的位置。

paraphrase.explain: 统计雕像位置由“南门附近”移到“睡莲池正北、几乎公园中心”，即地图上的 E。

pairs.note: 题干与原文同词，直接定位。

pairs.p: on the river bank, where the path from the east gate makes a sharp bend
pairs.note: 位于河岸边、东门小路的急转弯处，对应地图上的 C。

paraphrase.explain: 木雕区在河岸边、东门小路急弯处，即地图上的 C。

pairs.p: between the river and the path that leads from the pond to the river
pairs.note: 在河与从池塘通往河边的小路之间，对应地图上的 B。

paraphrase.explain: 扩建后的游乐场夹在河和“从池塘到河边”的小路之间，即地图上的 B。

pairs.note: 题干问迷宫位置，原文说“靠近西门”。

pairs.p: you go north from there towards the river and then turn left to reach it
pairs.note: 从西门朝河边（往北）走再左转，就到了 A 的位置。

paraphrase.explain: 迷宫在西门附近、往河边方向再左转即到，对应地图 A。

pairs.p: in the south-west corner of the park
pairs.note: 题干问网球场位置，原文说仍在公园西南角。

pairs.p: where there's a right-angle bend in the path
pairs.note: 小路呈直角转弯的地方，对应地图上西南角的 G。

paraphrase.explain: 网球场保留在公园西南角、小路直角转弯处，即地图上的 G。

pairs.p: right next to the lily pond on the same side as the west gate
pairs.note: 健身区就在睡莲池旁、与西门同侧，对应地图 D。

paraphrase.explain: 新健身区紧挨睡莲池、与西门在同侧（西边），即地图上的 D。
